# EventPlanning Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                | Function Name          | HTTP Methods | Template File          | Context Variables                                                                                                                                                                    |
|---------------------------|------------------------|--------------|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `/`                       | `root_redirect`         | GET          | None (redirect)         | None                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `/dashboard`              | `dashboard`             | GET          | `dashboard.html`        | `featured_events` (List[Dict]): List of event dicts with keys: `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str, YYYY-MM-DD), and `location` (str)                                                                 | 
|                           |                        |              |                        | 
| `/events`                 | `events_listing`        | GET          | `events.html`            | `events` (List[Dict]): List of event dicts with fields: `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int)
|                           |                        |              |                        | `categories` (List[str]): List of distinct event categories (e.g., Conference, Concert, Sports, Workshop, Social)                                                                      |
| `/events/search`           | `search_events`         | POST         | `events.html`            | Same as `/events` for post-search rendering: `events` filtered list, `categories` as above                                                                                          |
| `/event/<int:event_id>`    | `event_details`         | GET          | `event_details.html`     | `event` (Dict): dict with keys `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `time` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int) |
| `/book_ticket`            | `book_ticket`           | GET, POST    | `ticket_booking.html`    | GET: `events` (List[Dict]) with each dict containing `event_id` (int) and `event_name` (str)
POST: `confirmation` (Dict): booking confirmation details including `event_name` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `ticket_type` (str), `total_amount` (float), `booking_status` (str) |
| `/participants`           | `participants_management`| GET, POST    | `participants.html`      | GET: `participants` (List[Dict]) with keys: `participant_id` (int), `name` (str), `email` (str), `event_name` (str), `status` (str)
POST: updated `participants` list as above after additions or search filtering                                                 |
| `/venues`                 | `venues`                | GET          | `venues.html`            | `venues` (List[Dict]): List with keys: `venue_id` (int), `venue_name` (str), `location` (str), `capacity` (int), `amenities` (str), `contact` (str)                                 |
| `/event_schedules`        | `event_schedules`       | GET          | `schedules.html`         | `schedules` (List[Dict]): List with keys: `schedule_id` (int), `event_id` (int), `session_title` (str), `session_time` (str), `duration_minutes` (int), `speaker` (str), `venue_id` (int)
`events` (List[Dict]): List for filtering with `event_id` and `event_name`                                                    |
| `/bookings`               | `bookings_summary`      | GET          | `bookings.html`          | `bookings` (List[Dict]): List with keys: `booking_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `status` (str)                                                |
| `/booking/cancel/<int:booking_id>` | `cancel_booking`        | POST         | None (redirect)          | Redirects to `/bookings` after cancellation; no context variables                                                                                                                  |


### Route Details and Notes

- The root route `/` redirects to `/dashboard`, which renders the Dashboard page.
- Actions such as ticket booking submission and participant additions use POST methods.
- Dynamic routes include `/event/<int:event_id>` for event details and `/booking/cancel/<int:booking_id>` to cancel bookings.
- Context variables are carefully structured for template rendering and include lists of dictionaries or single dictionaries as needed.

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Template File: `templates/dashboard.html`
- Page Title: "Event Planning Dashboard" (used in `<title>` and `<h1>`)
- Element IDs:
  - `dashboard-page` (Div): Container for dashboard content
  - `featured-events` (Div): Displays featured events list
  - `browse-events-button` (Button): Navigates to `/events`
  - `view-tickets-button` (Button): Navigates to `/bookings`
  - `venues-button` (Button): Navigates to `/venues`
- Context Variables:
  - `featured_events` (List of dicts): Each dict has `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str)
- Navigation Buttons:
  - `browse-events-button`: `url_for('events_listing')`
  - `view-tickets-button`: `url_for('bookings_summary')`
  - `venues-button`: `url_for('venues')`

### 2. Events Listing Page
- Template File: `templates/events.html`
- Page Title: "Events Catalog" (in `<title>` and `<h1>`)
- Element IDs:
  - `events-page` (Div): Container for events list
  - `event-search-input` (Input): Search input for event name/location/date
  - `event-category-filter` (Dropdown): Categories filter dropdown
  - `events-grid` (Div): Container displaying event cards
  - Dynamic: `view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}` (Button): Button on each event card to view details
- Context Variables:
  - `events` (List of dicts): Each with fields `event_id`, `event_name`, `category`, `date`, `location`, `description`, `venue_id`, `capacity`
  - `categories` (List[str]): Event categories
- Navigation Buttons:
  - No global navigation buttons required here but navigation to event details is via dynamic buttons using `url_for('event_details', event_id=event.event_id)`
- Rendering instructions:
  - Render `events-grid` by looping over `events` with Jinja2 `{% for event in events %}`
  - Assign element ID: `id="view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}"`

### 3. Event Details Page
- Template File: `templates/event_details.html`
- Page Title: "Event Details"
- Element IDs:
  - `event-details-page` (Div): Container
  - `event-title` (H1): Event title
  - `event-date` (Div): Event date and time
  - `event-location` (Div): Event location
  - `event-description` (Div): Event detailed description
  - `book-ticket-button` (Button): To initiate ticket booking for this event
- Context Variables:
  - `event` (Dict): keys as noted in Section 1
- Navigation Buttons:
  - `book-ticket-button` navigates to `/book_ticket` with perhaps event selection pre-filled via query parameters or form (implementation detail: include event_id in form or URL)

### 4. Ticket Booking Page
- Template File: `templates/ticket_booking.html`
- Page Title: "Book Your Tickets"
- Element IDs:
  - `ticket-booking-page` (Div): Container
  - `select-event-dropdown` (Dropdown): Select event to book (options from `events`)
  - `ticket-quantity-input` (Input number): Number of tickets input
  - `ticket-type-select` (Dropdown): Ticket types General, VIP, Early Bird
  - `book-now-button` (Button): Submit booking
  - `booking-confirmation` (Div): Show confirmation details post-booking
- Context Variables:
  - GET: `events` (List[Dict]) with `event_id`, `event_name`
  - POST: `confirmation` (Dict) with keys: `event_name` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `ticket_type` (str), `total_amount` (float), `booking_status` (str)
- Navigation:
  - On POST success, display confirmation inside `booking-confirmation`

### 5. Participants Management Page
- Template File: `templates/participants.html`
- Page Title: "Participants Management"
- Element IDs:
  - `participants-page` (Div): Container
  - `participants-table` (Table): Displays participant info columns
  - `add-participant-button` (Button): Opens form to add participant
  - `search-participant-input` (Input): Search field
  - `participant-status-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by status
- Context Variables:
  - `participants` (List[Dict]): Each dict with keys: `participant_id` (int), `name` (str), `email` (str), `event_name` (str), `status` (str)
- Navigation:
  - Adding participant via `add-participant-button` triggers a POST or modal

### 6. Venue Information Page
- Template File: `templates/venues.html`
- Page Title: "Venues"
- Element IDs:
  - `venues-page` (Div): Container
  - `venues-grid` (Div): Grid showing venue cards
  - `venue-search-input` (Input): Search venues by name/location
  - `venue-capacity-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by capacity (Small, Medium, Large)
  - Dynamic: `view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}` (Button): View venue details
- Context Variables:
  - `venues` (List[Dict]): with `venue_id`, `venue_name`, `location`, `capacity`, `amenities`, `contact`
- Navigation:
  - Dynamic buttons route to venue detail pages or modals (not specified, so link or JS actions)
  - Example dynamic ID rendering with `{% for venue in venues %}` and `id="view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}"`

### 7. Event Schedules Page
- Template File: `templates/schedules.html`
- Page Title: "Event Schedules"
- Element IDs:
  - `schedules-page` (Div): Container
  - `schedules-timeline` (Div): Timeline view container
  - `schedule-filter-date` (Input date): Date filter
  - `schedule-filter-event` (Dropdown): Event filter
  - `export-schedule-button` (Button): Export schedule data
- Context Variables:
  - `schedules` (List[Dict]): with schedule details
  - `events` (List[Dict]): for filter dropdown
- Navigation:
  - Filtering triggers page updates
  - Export button triggers schedule data export

### 8. Bookings Summary Page
- Template File: `templates/bookings.html`
- Page Title: "My Bookings"
- Element IDs:
  - `bookings-page` (Div): Container
  - `bookings-table` (Table): List bookings with columns: event, date, ticket count, status
  - `booking-search-input` (Input): Search by event or booking ID
  - Dynamic: `cancel-booking-button-{{ booking.booking_id }}` (Button): Cancel booking
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button): Navigate back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - `bookings` (List[Dict]): each with `booking_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `status` (str)
- Navigation:
  - `back-to-dashboard` button uses `url_for('dashboard')`
  - Cancel buttons POST to `/booking/cancel/<booking_id>` handled by backend

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas

### 1. Events Data
- File Path: `data/events.txt`
- Fields (pipe-delimited): 
  `event_id|event_name|category|date|time|location|description|venue_id|capacity`
- Description: Stores event details including scheduling and venue link.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Tech Conference 2025|Conference|2025-03-15|09:00|Convention Center, San Francisco|Annual technology conference discussing latest innovations|1|500
  2|Summer Music Festival|Concert|2025-06-20|18:00|Central Park, New York|Three-day music festival with international artists|2|5000
  3|Marathon Championship|Sports|2025-04-10|07:00|Downtown Loop, Chicago|Annual city marathon event for all skill levels|3|1000
  ```

### 2. Venues Data
- File Path: `data/venues.txt`
- Fields:
  `venue_id|venue_name|location|capacity|amenities|contact`
- Description: Stores venue information including amenities and contact.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Convention Center|San Francisco, CA|500|WiFi, Parking, Catering|contact@convention.com
  2|Central Park|New York, NY|5000|Outdoor Space, Restrooms, Food Vendors|info@centralparkevents.org
  3|Downtown Loop|Chicago, IL|1000|Paved Surface, Water Stations, Medical Support|events@downtown.com
  ```

### 3. Tickets Data
- File Path: `data/tickets.txt`
- Fields:
  `ticket_id|event_id|ticket_type|price|available_count|sold_count`
- Description: Stores ticket types and inventory counts per event.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|General|49.99|500|150
  2|1|VIP|99.99|100|45
  3|2|Early Bird|39.99|1000|750
  ```

### 4. Bookings Data
- File Path: `data/bookings.txt`
- Fields:
  `booking_id|event_id|customer_name|booking_date|ticket_count|ticket_type|total_amount|status`
- Description: Records user ticket bookings with status.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|2025-02-01|2|General|99.98|Confirmed
  2|2|Bob Williams|2025-02-05|4|Early Bird|159.96|Confirmed
  3|1|Carol Davis|2025-02-10|1|VIP|99.99|Pending
  ```

### 5. Participants Data
- File Path: `data/participants.txt`
- Fields:
  `participant_id|event_id|name|email|booking_id|status|registration_date`
- Description: Stores participant personal and attendance information per event.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|alice@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  2|1|Michael Johnson|michael@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  3|2|Bob Williams|bob@email.com|2|Attended|2025-02-05
  ```

### 6. Schedules Data
- File Path: `data/schedules.txt`
- Fields:
  `schedule_id|event_id|session_title|session_time|duration_minutes|speaker|venue_id`
- Description: Stores event schedule sessions with timing and speaker details.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Opening Keynote|2025-03-15 09:00|60|John Smith|1
  2|1|Panel Discussion|2025-03-15 10:30|90|Expert Panel|1
  3|2|Headliner Performance|2025-06-20 20:00|120|International Artist|2
  ```


---

This design specification provides detailed routes for Flask backend, precise HTML template requirements, and strict data file schemas for frontend and backend implementation of the 'EventPlanning' application. No additional features or assumptions beyond requirements are included.

# EventPlanning Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                | Function Name          | HTTP Methods | Template File          | Context Variables                                                                                                                                                                    |
|---------------------------|------------------------|--------------|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `/`                       | `root_redirect`         | GET          | None (redirect)         | None                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `/dashboard`              | `dashboard`             | GET          | `dashboard.html`        | `featured_events` (List[Dict]): List of event dicts with keys: `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str, YYYY-MM-DD), and `location` (str)                                                                 | 
|                           |                        |              |                        | 
| `/events`                 | `events_listing`        | GET          | `events.html`            | `events` (List[Dict]): List of event dicts with fields: `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int)
|                           |                        |              |                        | `categories` (List[str]): List of distinct event categories (e.g., Conference, Concert, Sports, Workshop, Social)                                                                      |
| `/events/search`           | `search_events`         | POST         | `events.html`            | Same as `/events` for post-search rendering: `events` filtered list, `categories` as above                                                                                          |
| `/event/<int:event_id>`    | `event_details`         | GET          | `event_details.html`     | `event` (Dict): dict with keys `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `time` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int) |
| `/book_ticket`            | `book_ticket`           | GET, POST    | `ticket_booking.html`    | GET: `events` (List[Dict]) with each dict containing `event_id` (int) and `event_name` (str)
POST: `confirmation` (Dict): booking confirmation details including `event_name` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `ticket_type` (str), `total_amount` (float), `booking_status` (str) |
| `/participants`           | `participants_management`| GET, POST    | `participants.html`      | GET: `participants` (List[Dict]) with keys: `participant_id` (int), `name` (str), `email` (str), `event_name` (str), `status` (str)
POST: updated `participants` list as above after additions or search filtering                                                 |
| `/venues`                 | `venues`                | GET          | `venues.html`            | `venues` (List[Dict]): List with keys: `venue_id` (int), `venue_name` (str), `location` (str), `capacity` (int), `amenities` (str), `contact` (str)                                 |
| `/event_schedules`        | `event_schedules`       | GET          | `schedules.html`         | `schedules` (List[Dict]): List with keys: `schedule_id` (int), `event_id` (int), `session_title` (str), `session_time` (str), `duration_minutes` (int), `speaker` (str), `venue_id` (int)
`events` (List[Dict]): List for filtering with `event_id` and `event_name`                                                    |
| `/bookings`               | `bookings_summary`      | GET          | `bookings.html`          | `bookings` (List[Dict]): List with keys: `booking_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `status` (str)                                                |
| `/booking/cancel/<int:booking_id>` | `cancel_booking`        | POST         | None (redirect)          | Redirects to `/bookings` after cancellation; no context variables                                                                                                                  |


### Route Details and Notes

- The root route `/` redirects to `/dashboard`, which renders the Dashboard page.
- Actions such as ticket booking submission and participant additions use POST methods.
- Dynamic routes include `/event/<int:event_id>` for event details and `/booking/cancel/<int:booking_id>` to cancel bookings.
- Context variables are carefully structured for template rendering and include lists of dictionaries or single dictionaries as needed.

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Template File: `templates/dashboard.html`
- Page Title: "Event Planning Dashboard" (used in `<title>` and `<h1>`)
- Element IDs:
  - `dashboard-page` (Div): Container for dashboard content
  - `featured-events` (Div): Displays featured events list
  - `browse-events-button` (Button): Navigates to `/events`
  - `view-tickets-button` (Button): Navigates to `/bookings`
  - `venues-button` (Button): Navigates to `/venues`
- Context Variables:
  - `featured_events` (List of dicts): Each dict has `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str)
- Navigation Buttons:
  - `browse-events-button`: `url_for('events_listing')`
  - `view-tickets-button`: `url_for('bookings_summary')`
  - `venues-button`: `url_for('venues')`

### 2. Events Listing Page
- Template File: `templates/events.html`
- Page Title: "Events Catalog" (in `<title>` and `<h1>`)
- Element IDs:
  - `events-page` (Div): Container for events list
  - `event-search-input` (Input): Search input for event name/location/date
  - `event-category-filter` (Dropdown): Categories filter dropdown
  - `events-grid` (Div): Container displaying event cards
  - Dynamic: `view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}` (Button): Button on each event card to view details
- Context Variables:
  - `events` (List of dicts): Each with fields `event_id`, `event_name`, `category`, `date`, `location`, `description`, `venue_id`, `capacity`
  - `categories` (List[str]): Event categories
- Navigation Buttons:
  - No global navigation buttons required here but navigation to event details is via dynamic buttons using `url_for('event_details', event_id=event.event_id)`
- Rendering instructions:
  - Render `events-grid` by looping over `events` with Jinja2 `{% for event in events %}`
  - Assign element ID: `id="view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}"`

### 3. Event Details Page
- Template File: `templates/event_details.html`
- Page Title: "Event Details"
- Element IDs:
  - `event-details-page` (Div): Container
  - `event-title` (H1): Event title
  - `event-date` (Div): Event date and time
  - `event-location` (Div): Event location
  - `event-description` (Div): Event detailed description
  - `book-ticket-button` (Button): To initiate ticket booking for this event
- Context Variables:
  - `event` (Dict): keys as noted in Section 1
- Navigation Buttons:
  - `book-ticket-button` navigates to `/book_ticket` with perhaps event selection pre-filled via query parameters or form (implementation detail: include event_id in form or URL)

### 4. Ticket Booking Page
- Template File: `templates/ticket_booking.html`
- Page Title: "Book Your Tickets"
- Element IDs:
  - `ticket-booking-page` (Div): Container
  - `select-event-dropdown` (Dropdown): Select event to book (options from `events`)
  - `ticket-quantity-input` (Input number): Number of tickets input
  - `ticket-type-select` (Dropdown): Ticket types General, VIP, Early Bird
  - `book-now-button` (Button): Submit booking
  - `booking-confirmation` (Div): Show confirmation details post-booking
- Context Variables:
  - GET: `events` (List[Dict]) with `event_id`, `event_name`
  - POST: `confirmation` (Dict) with keys: `event_name` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `ticket_type` (str), `total_amount` (float), `booking_status` (str)
- Navigation:
  - On POST success, display confirmation inside `booking-confirmation`

### 5. Participants Management Page
- Template File: `templates/participants.html`
- Page Title: "Participants Management"
- Element IDs:
  - `participants-page` (Div): Container
  - `participants-table` (Table): Displays participant info columns
  - `add-participant-button` (Button): Opens form to add participant
  - `search-participant-input` (Input): Search field
  - `participant-status-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by status
- Context Variables:
  - `participants` (List[Dict]): Each dict with keys: `participant_id` (int), `name` (str), `email` (str), `event_name` (str), `status` (str)
- Navigation:
  - Adding participant via `add-participant-button` triggers a POST or modal

### 6. Venue Information Page
- Template File: `templates/venues.html`
- Page Title: "Venues"
- Element IDs:
  - `venues-page` (Div): Container
  - `venues-grid` (Div): Grid showing venue cards
  - `venue-search-input` (Input): Search venues by name/location
  - `venue-capacity-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by capacity (Small, Medium, Large)
  - Dynamic: `view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}` (Button): View venue details
- Context Variables:
  - `venues` (List[Dict]): with `venue_id`, `venue_name`, `location`, `capacity`, `amenities`, `contact`
- Navigation:
  - Dynamic buttons route to venue detail pages or modals (not specified, so link or JS actions)
  - Example dynamic ID rendering with `{% for venue in venues %}` and `id="view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}"`

### 7. Event Schedules Page
- Template File: `templates/schedules.html`
- Page Title: "Event Schedules"
- Element IDs:
  - `schedules-page` (Div): Container
  - `schedules-timeline` (Div): Timeline view container
  - `schedule-filter-date` (Input date): Date filter
  - `schedule-filter-event` (Dropdown): Event filter
  - `export-schedule-button` (Button): Export schedule data
- Context Variables:
  - `schedules` (List[Dict]): with schedule details
  - `events` (List[Dict]): for filter dropdown
- Navigation:
  - Filtering triggers page updates
  - Export button triggers schedule data export

### 8. Bookings Summary Page
- Template File: `templates/bookings.html`
- Page Title: "My Bookings"
- Element IDs:
  - `bookings-page` (Div): Container
  - `bookings-table` (Table): List bookings with columns: event, date, ticket count, status
  - `booking-search-input` (Input): Search by event or booking ID
  - Dynamic: `cancel-booking-button-{{ booking.booking_id }}` (Button): Cancel booking
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button): Navigate back to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - `bookings` (List[Dict]): each with `booking_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `status` (str)
- Navigation:
  - `back-to-dashboard` button uses `url_for('dashboard')`
  - Cancel buttons POST to `/booking/cancel/<booking_id>` handled by backend

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas

### 1. Events Data
- File Path: `data/events.txt`
- Fields (pipe-delimited): 
  `event_id|event_name|category|date|time|location|description|venue_id|capacity`
- Description: Stores event details including scheduling and venue link.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Tech Conference 2025|Conference|2025-03-15|09:00|Convention Center, San Francisco|Annual technology conference discussing latest innovations|1|500
  2|Summer Music Festival|Concert|2025-06-20|18:00|Central Park, New York|Three-day music festival with international artists|2|5000
  3|Marathon Championship|Sports|2025-04-10|07:00|Downtown Loop, Chicago|Annual city marathon event for all skill levels|3|1000
  ```

### 2. Venues Data
- File Path: `data/venues.txt`
- Fields:
  `venue_id|venue_name|location|capacity|amenities|contact`
- Description: Stores venue information including amenities and contact.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Convention Center|San Francisco, CA|500|WiFi, Parking, Catering|contact@convention.com
  2|Central Park|New York, NY|5000|Outdoor Space, Restrooms, Food Vendors|info@centralparkevents.org
  3|Downtown Loop|Chicago, IL|1000|Paved Surface, Water Stations, Medical Support|events@downtown.com
  ```

### 3. Tickets Data
- File Path: `data/tickets.txt`
- Fields:
  `ticket_id|event_id|ticket_type|price|available_count|sold_count`
- Description: Stores ticket types and inventory counts per event.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|General|49.99|500|150
  2|1|VIP|99.99|100|45
  3|2|Early Bird|39.99|1000|750
  ```

### 4. Bookings Data
- File Path: `data/bookings.txt`
- Fields:
  `booking_id|event_id|customer_name|booking_date|ticket_count|ticket_type|total_amount|status`
- Description: Records user ticket bookings with status.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|2025-02-01|2|General|99.98|Confirmed
  2|2|Bob Williams|2025-02-05|4|Early Bird|159.96|Confirmed
  3|1|Carol Davis|2025-02-10|1|VIP|99.99|Pending
  ```

### 5. Participants Data
- File Path: `data/participants.txt`
- Fields:
  `participant_id|event_id|name|email|booking_id|status|registration_date`
- Description: Stores participant personal and attendance information per event.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|alice@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  2|1|Michael Johnson|michael@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  3|2|Bob Williams|bob@email.com|2|Attended|2025-02-05
  ```

### 6. Schedules Data
- File Path: `data/schedules.txt`
- Fields:
  `schedule_id|event_id|session_title|session_time|duration_minutes|speaker|venue_id`
- Description: Stores event schedule sessions with timing and speaker details.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Opening Keynote|2025-03-15 09:00|60|John Smith|1
  2|1|Panel Discussion|2025-03-15 10:30|90|Expert Panel|1
  3|2|Headliner Performance|2025-06-20 20:00|120|International Artist|2
  ```


---

This design specification provides detailed routes for Flask backend, precise HTML template requirements, and strict data file schemas for frontend and backend implementation of the 'EventPlanning' application. No additional features or assumptions beyond requirements are included.

# EventPlanning Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                   | Function Name             | HTTP Methods | Template File            | Context Variables                                                                                                                                                |
|------------------------------|---------------------------|--------------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `/`                          | `root_redirect`            | GET          | None (redirect)           | None                                                                                                                                                           |
| `/dashboard`                 | `dashboard`                | GET          | `dashboard.html`          | `featured_events`: List[Dict] - Each dict containing `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str)                                        |
| `/events`                   | `events_listing`           | GET          | `events.html`              | `events`: List[Dict] - Each dict with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int)
|                              |                           |              |                          | `categories`: List[str] - List of event categories ("Conference", "Concert", "Sports", "Workshop", "Social")                                            |
| `/events/search`             | `search_events`            | POST         | `events.html`              | Same as `/events` after filtering/search: updated `events` list, `categories`                                                                            |
| `/event/<int:event_id>`      | `event_details`            | GET          | `event_details.html`       | `event`: Dict with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `time` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int) |
| `/book_ticket`              | `book_ticket`              | GET, POST    | `ticket_booking.html`      | GET: `events` List[Dict] with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str)
POST: `confirmation` Dict with keys: `event_name` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `ticket_type` (str), `total_amount` (float), `booking_status` (str)     |
| `/participants`             | `participants_management`  | GET, POST    | `participants.html`        | `participants`: List[Dict] with `participant_id` (int), `name` (str), `email` (str), `event_name` (str), `status` (str)
POST: updated filtered list of participants                                                               |
| `/venues`                   | `venues`                   | GET          | `venues.html`              | `venues`: List[Dict] with `venue_id` (int), `venue_name` (str), `location` (str), `capacity` (int), `amenities` (str), `contact` (str)                         |
| `/event_schedules`          | `event_schedules`          | GET          | `schedules.html`           | `schedules`: List[Dict] with `schedule_id` (int), `event_id` (int), `session_title` (str), `session_time` (str), `duration_minutes` (int), `speaker` (str), `venue_id` (int)
`events`: List[Dict] for filters with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str)                                   |
| `/bookings`                 | `bookings_summary`         | GET          | `bookings.html`            | `bookings`: List[Dict] with `booking_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `status` (str)                                        |
| `/booking/cancel/<int:booking_id>` | `cancel_booking`            | POST         | None (redirect)            | POST redirect to `/bookings` after cancellation; no context variables                                                                                        |


**Notes:**
- Root `/` redirects to `/dashboard` per requirements.
- POST methods used for form submissions: event search, participant management, booking cancellation.
- Dynamic routes for event details and booking cancellation.
- Context variables named with consistent snake_case.

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Template File: `templates/dashboard.html`
- Page Title: "Event Planning Dashboard" (used in `<title>` and `<h1>`)
- Element IDs:
  - `dashboard-page` (Div): Container for the dashboard
  - `featured-events` (Div): Shows featured event recommendations
  - `browse-events-button` (Button): Navigate to `/events`
  - `view-tickets-button` (Button): Navigate to `/bookings`
  - `venues-button` (Button): Navigate to `/venues`
- Context Variables:
  - `featured_events`: List[Dict] with fields: `event_id`, `event_name`, `date`, `location`
- Navigation Buttons:
  - `browse-events-button`: links to `url_for('events_listing')`
  - `view-tickets-button`: links to `url_for('bookings_summary')`
  - `venues-button`: links to `url_for('venues')`

### 2. Events Listing Page
- Template File: `templates/events.html`
- Page Title: "Events Catalog"
- Element IDs:
  - `events-page` (Div): Container for events list
  - `event-search-input` (Input): Search field for event name, location, date
  - `event-category-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by category (Conference, Concert, Sports, Workshop, Social)
  - `events-grid` (Div): Grid container for event cards
  - Dynamic Button IDs: `view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}` (Button) on each event card for event details
- Context Variables:
  - `events`: List[Dict] of events with full event info
  - `categories`: List[str] for category filter dropdown
- Navigation:
  - Dynamic buttons link to event details page: `url_for('event_details', event_id=event.event_id)`
- Rendering instructions:
  - Use Jinja2 loop `{% for event in events %}` to populate `events-grid`
  - Assign dynamic button ID: `id="view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}"`

### 3. Event Details Page
- Template File: `templates/event_details.html`
- Page Title: "Event Details"
- Element IDs:
  - `event-details-page` (Div): Container
  - `event-title` (H1): Event name
  - `event-date` (Div): Event date and time
  - `event-location` (Div): Event location
  - `event-description` (Div): Detailed event description
  - `book-ticket-button` (Button): Navigate to ticket booking
- Context Variables:
  - `event`: Dict with full event details
- Navigation:
  - `book-ticket-button`: should link to `/book_ticket` or submit form with selected event

### 4. Ticket Booking Page
- Template File: `templates/ticket_booking.html`
- Page Title: "Book Your Tickets"
- Element IDs:
  - `ticket-booking-page` (Div): Container
  - `select-event-dropdown` (Dropdown): Event selection
  - `ticket-quantity-input` (Input number): Input for number of tickets
  - `ticket-type-select` (Dropdown): Ticket type selection (General, VIP, Early Bird)
  - `book-now-button` (Button): Submit booking
  - `booking-confirmation` (Div): Show confirmation details
- Context Variables:
  - GET: `events` List[Dict] with `event_id`, `event_name`
  - POST: `confirmation` Dict with keys: `event_name`, `ticket_count`, `ticket_type`, `total_amount`, `booking_status`
- Navigation:
  - Upon POST successful booking, display `booking-confirmation` content

### 5. Participants Management Page
- Template File: `templates/participants.html`
- Page Title: "Participants Management"
- Element IDs:
  - `participants-page` (Div): Container
  - `participants-table` (Table): List participant data columns: name, email, event, status
  - `add-participant-button` (Button): Add new participant action
  - `search-participant-input` (Input): Search field
  - `participant-status-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by status (Registered, Confirmed, Attended)
- Context Variables:
  - `participants`: List[Dict] with participant info
- Navigation:
  - `add-participant-button` triggers participant add form POST

### 6. Venue Information Page
- Template File: `templates/venues.html`
- Page Title: "Venues"
- Element IDs:
  - `venues-page` (Div): Container
  - `venues-grid` (Div): Venue cards container
  - `venue-search-input` (Input): Search venues
  - `venue-capacity-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by capacity (Small, Medium, Large)
  - Dynamic Button IDs: `view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}` (Button) to view venue details
- Context Variables:
  - `venues`: List[Dict] with all venue details
- Navigation:
  - Dynamic view buttons link or trigger venue detail views
- Rendering instructions:
  - Use Jinja2 `{% for venue in venues %}` loop
  - Assign button ID `id="view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}"`

### 7. Event Schedules Page
- Template File: `templates/schedules.html`
- Page Title: "Event Schedules"
- Element IDs:
  - `schedules-page` (Div): Container
  - `schedules-timeline` (Div): Timeline display
  - `schedule-filter-date` (Input date): Date filter
  - `schedule-filter-event` (Dropdown): Event filter
  - `export-schedule-button` (Button): Export schedule
- Context Variables:
  - `schedules`: List[Dict] with schedule session details
  - `events`: List[Dict] for event filter
- Navigation:
  - Filters trigger page filtering
  - Export triggers schedule export

### 8. Bookings Summary Page
- Template File: `templates/bookings.html`
- Page Title: "My Bookings"
- Element IDs:
  - `bookings-page` (Div): Container
  - `bookings-table` (Table): Lists bookings
  - `booking-search-input` (Input): Search field
  - Dynamic Button IDs: `cancel-booking-button-{{ booking.booking_id }}` (Button) to cancel booking
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button): Navigate to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - `bookings`: List[Dict] with booking info
- Navigation:
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`
  - Cancel buttons POST to `/booking/cancel/<booking_id>`

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas

### 1. Events Data
- File Path: `data/events.txt`
- Fields (pipe-delimited):
  ```
  event_id|event_name|category|date|time|location|description|venue_id|capacity
  ```
- Description: Stores event information including schedule, location, and capacity related to a venue.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Tech Conference 2025|Conference|2025-03-15|09:00|Convention Center, San Francisco|Annual technology conference discussing latest innovations|1|500
  2|Summer Music Festival|Concert|2025-06-20|18:00|Central Park, New York|Three-day music festival with international artists|2|5000
  3|Marathon Championship|Sports|2025-04-10|07:00|Downtown Loop, Chicago|Annual city marathon event for all skill levels|3|1000
  ```

### 2. Venues Data
- File Path: `data/venues.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  venue_id|venue_name|location|capacity|amenities|contact
  ```
- Description: Contains venue details including amenities and contact info.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Convention Center|San Francisco, CA|500|WiFi, Parking, Catering|contact@convention.com
  2|Central Park|New York, NY|5000|Outdoor Space, Restrooms, Food Vendors|info@centralparkevents.org
  3|Downtown Loop|Chicago, IL|1000|Paved Surface, Water Stations, Medical Support|events@downtown.com
  ```

### 3. Tickets Data
- File Path: `data/tickets.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  ticket_id|event_id|ticket_type|price|available_count|sold_count
  ```
- Description: Ticket inventory and prices for event ticket types.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|General|49.99|500|150
  2|1|VIP|99.99|100|45
  3|2|Early Bird|39.99|1000|750
  ```

### 4. Bookings Data
- File Path: `data/bookings.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  booking_id|event_id|customer_name|booking_date|ticket_count|ticket_type|total_amount|status
  ```
- Description: Stores booking records including customer details and booking status.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|2025-02-01|2|General|99.98|Confirmed
  2|2|Bob Williams|2025-02-05|4|Early Bird|159.96|Confirmed
  3|1|Carol Davis|2025-02-10|1|VIP|99.99|Pending
  ```

### 5. Participants Data
- File Path: `data/participants.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  participant_id|event_id|name|email|booking_id|status|registration_date
  ```
- Description: Participant records with attendance status and related booking
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|alice@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  2|1|Michael Johnson|michael@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  3|2|Bob Williams|bob@email.com|2|Attended|2025-02-05
  ```

### 6. Schedules Data
- File Path: `data/schedules.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  schedule_id|event_id|session_title|session_time|duration_minutes|speaker|venue_id
  ```
- Description: Stores schedule sessions with timing, speakers, linked to events and venues
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Opening Keynote|2025-03-15 09:00|60|John Smith|1
  2|1|Panel Discussion|2025-03-15 10:30|90|Expert Panel|1
  3|2|Headliner Performance|2025-06-20 20:00|120|International Artist|2
  ```

---

This design specification strictly adheres to the user requirements. It enables independent frontend and backend development without assumptions or undocumented features.

log.before:
# EventPlanning Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                   | Function Name             | HTTP Methods | Template File            | Context Variables                                                                                                                                                |
|------------------------------|---------------------------|--------------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `/`                          | `root_redirect`            | GET          | None (redirect)           | None                                                                                                                                                           |
| `/dashboard`                 | `dashboard`                | GET          | `dashboard.html`          | `featured_events`: List[Dict] - Each dict containing `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str)                                        |
| `/events`                   | `events_listing`           | GET          | `events.html`              | `events`: List[Dict] - Each dict with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int)
|                              |                           |              |                          | `categories`: List[str] - List of event categories ("Conference", "Concert", "Sports", "Workshop", "Social")                                            |
| `/events/search`             | `search_events`            | POST         | `events.html`              | Same as `/events` after filtering/search: updated `events` list, `categories`                                                                            |
| `/event/<int:event_id>`      | `event_details`            | GET          | `event_details.html`       | `event`: Dict with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `category` (str), `date` (str), `time` (str), `location` (str), `description` (str), `venue_id` (int), `capacity` (int) |
| `/book_ticket`              | `book_ticket`              | GET, POST    | `ticket_booking.html`      | GET: `events` List[Dict] with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str)
POST: `confirmation` Dict with keys: `event_name` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `ticket_type` (str), `total_amount` (float), `booking_status` (str)     |
| `/participants`             | `participants_management`  | GET, POST    | `participants.html`        | `participants`: List[Dict] with `participant_id` (int), `name` (str), `email` (str), `event_name` (str), `status` (str)
POST: updated filtered list of participants                                                               |
| `/venues`                   | `venues`                   | GET          | `venues.html`              | `venues`: List[Dict] with `venue_id` (int), `venue_name` (str), `location` (str), `capacity` (int), `amenities` (str), `contact` (str)                         |
| `/event_schedules`          | `event_schedules`          | GET          | `schedules.html`           | `schedules`: List[Dict] with `schedule_id` (int), `event_id` (int), `session_title` (str), `session_time` (str), `duration_minutes` (int), `speaker` (str), `venue_id` (int)
`events`: List[Dict] for filters with `event_id` (int), `event_name` (str)                                   |
| `/bookings`                 | `bookings_summary`         | GET          | `bookings.html`            | `bookings`: List[Dict] with `booking_id` (int), `event_name` (str), `date` (str), `ticket_count` (int), `status` (str)                                        |
| `/booking/cancel/<int:booking_id>` | `cancel_booking`            | POST         | None (redirect)            | POST redirect to `/bookings` after cancellation; no context variables                                                                                        |


**Notes:**
- Root `/` redirects to `/dashboard` per requirements.
- POST methods used for form submissions: event search, participant management, booking cancellation.
- Dynamic routes for event details and booking cancellation.
- Context variables named with consistent snake_case.

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Template File: `templates/dashboard.html`
- Page Title: "Event Planning Dashboard" (used in `<title>` and `<h1>`)
- Element IDs:
  - `dashboard-page` (Div): Container for the dashboard
  - `featured-events` (Div): Shows featured event recommendations
  - `browse-events-button` (Button): Navigate to `/events`
  - `view-tickets-button` (Button): Navigate to `/bookings`
  - `venues-button` (Button): Navigate to `/venues`
- Context Variables:
  - `featured_events`: List[Dict] with fields: `event_id`, `event_name`, `date`, `location`
- Navigation Buttons:
  - `browse-events-button`: links to `url_for('events_listing')`
  - `view-tickets-button`: links to `url_for('bookings_summary')`
  - `venues-button`: links to `url_for('venues')`

### 2. Events Listing Page
- Template File: `templates/events.html`
- Page Title: "Events Catalog"
- Element IDs:
  - `events-page` (Div): Container for events list
  - `event-search-input` (Input): Search field for event name, location, date
  - `event-category-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by category (Conference, Concert, Sports, Workshop, Social)
  - `events-grid` (Div): Grid container for event cards
  - Dynamic Button IDs: `view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}` (Button) on each event card for event details
- Context Variables:
  - `events`: List[Dict] of events with full event info
  - `categories`: List[str] for category filter dropdown
- Navigation:
  - Dynamic buttons link to event details page: `url_for('event_details', event_id=event.event_id)`
- Rendering instructions:
  - Use Jinja2 loop `{% for event in events %}` to populate `events-grid`
  - Assign dynamic button ID: `id="view-event-button-{{ event.event_id }}"`

### 3. Event Details Page
- Template File: `templates/event_details.html`
- Page Title: "Event Details"
- Element IDs:
  - `event-details-page` (Div): Container
  - `event-title` (H1): Event name
  - `event-date` (Div): Event date and time
  - `event-location` (Div): Event location
  - `event-description` (Div): Detailed event description
  - `book-ticket-button` (Button): Navigate to ticket booking
- Context Variables:
  - `event`: Dict with full event details
- Navigation:
  - `book-ticket-button`: should link to `/book_ticket` or submit form with selected event

### 4. Ticket Booking Page
- Template File: `templates/ticket_booking.html`
- Page Title: "Book Your Tickets"
- Element IDs:
  - `ticket-booking-page` (Div): Container
  - `select-event-dropdown` (Dropdown): Event selection
  - `ticket-quantity-input` (Input number): Input for number of tickets
  - `ticket-type-select` (Dropdown): Ticket type selection (General, VIP, Early Bird)
  - `book-now-button` (Button): Submit booking
  - `booking-confirmation` (Div): Show confirmation details
- Context Variables:
  - GET: `events` List[Dict] with `event_id`, `event_name`
  - POST: `confirmation` Dict with keys: `event_name`, `ticket_count`, `ticket_type`, `total_amount`, `booking_status`
- Navigation:
  - Upon POST successful booking, display `booking-confirmation` content

### 5. Participants Management Page
- Template File: `templates/participants.html`
- Page Title: "Participants Management"
- Element IDs:
  - `participants-page` (Div): Container
  - `participants-table` (Table): List participant data columns: name, email, event, status
  - `add-participant-button` (Button): Add new participant action
  - `search-participant-input` (Input): Search field
  - `participant-status-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by status (Registered, Confirmed, Attended)
- Context Variables:
  - `participants`: List[Dict] with participant info
- Navigation:
  - `add-participant-button` triggers participant add form POST

### 6. Venue Information Page
- Template File: `templates/venues.html`
- Page Title: "Venues"
- Element IDs:
  - `venues-page` (Div): Container
  - `venues-grid` (Div): Venue cards container
  - `venue-search-input` (Input): Search venues
  - `venue-capacity-filter` (Dropdown): Filter by capacity (Small, Medium, Large)
  - Dynamic Button IDs: `view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}` (Button) to view venue details
- Context Variables:
  - `venues`: List[Dict] with all venue details
- Navigation:
  - Dynamic view buttons link or trigger venue detail views
- Rendering instructions:
  - Use Jinja2 `{% for venue in venues %}` loop
  - Assign button ID `id="view-venue-details-{{ venue.venue_id }}"`

### 7. Event Schedules Page
- Template File: `templates/schedules.html`
- Page Title: "Event Schedules"
- Element IDs:
  - `schedules-page` (Div): Container
  - `schedules-timeline` (Div): Timeline display
  - `schedule-filter-date` (Input date): Date filter
  - `schedule-filter-event` (Dropdown): Event filter
  - `export-schedule-button` (Button): Export schedule
- Context Variables:
  - `schedules`: List[Dict] with schedule session details
  - `events`: List[Dict] for event filter
- Navigation:
  - Filters trigger page filtering
  - Export triggers schedule export

### 8. Bookings Summary Page
- Template File: `templates/bookings.html`
- Page Title: "My Bookings"
- Element IDs:
  - `bookings-page` (Div): Container
  - `bookings-table` (Table): Lists bookings
  - `booking-search-input` (Input): Search field
  - Dynamic Button IDs: `cancel-booking-button-{{ booking.booking_id }}` (Button) to cancel booking
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button): Navigate to dashboard
- Context Variables:
  - `bookings`: List[Dict] with booking info
- Navigation:
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`
  - Cancel buttons POST to `/booking/cancel/<booking_id>`

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas

### 1. Events Data
- File Path: `data/events.txt`
- Fields (pipe-delimited):
  ```
  event_id|event_name|category|date|time|location|description|venue_id|capacity
  ```
- Description: Stores event information including schedule, location, and capacity related to a venue.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Tech Conference 2025|Conference|2025-03-15|09:00|Convention Center, San Francisco|Annual technology conference discussing latest innovations|1|500
  2|Summer Music Festival|Concert|2025-06-20|18:00|Central Park, New York|Three-day music festival with international artists|2|5000
  3|Marathon Championship|Sports|2025-04-10|07:00|Downtown Loop, Chicago|Annual city marathon event for all skill levels|3|1000
  ```

### 2. Venues Data
- File Path: `data/venues.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  venue_id|venue_name|location|capacity|amenities|contact
  ```
- Description: Contains venue details including amenities and contact info.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|Convention Center|San Francisco, CA|500|WiFi, Parking, Catering|contact@convention.com
  2|Central Park|New York, NY|5000|Outdoor Space, Restrooms, Food Vendors|info@centralparkevents.org
  3|Downtown Loop|Chicago, IL|1000|Paved Surface, Water Stations, Medical Support|events@downtown.com
  ```

### 3. Tickets Data
- File Path: `data/tickets.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  ticket_id|event_id|ticket_type|price|available_count|sold_count
  ```
- Description: Ticket inventory and prices for event ticket types.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|General|49.99|500|150
  2|1|VIP|99.99|100|45
  3|2|Early Bird|39.99|1000|750
  ```

### 4. Bookings Data
- File Path: `data/bookings.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  booking_id|event_id|customer_name|booking_date|ticket_count|ticket_type|total_amount|status
  ```
- Description: Stores booking records including customer details and booking status.
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|2025-02-01|2|General|99.98|Confirmed
  2|2|Bob Williams|2025-02-05|4|Early Bird|159.96|Confirmed
  3|1|Carol Davis|2025-02-10|1|VIP|99.99|Pending
  ```

### 5. Participants Data
- File Path: `data/participants.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  participant_id|event_id|name|email|booking_id|status|registration_date
  ```
- Description: Participant records with attendance status and related booking
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|alice@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  2|1|Michael Johnson|michael@email.com|1|Confirmed|2025-02-01
  3|2|Bob Williams|bob@email.com|2|Attended|2025-02-05
  ```

### 6. Schedules Data
- File Path: `data/schedules.txt`
- Fields:
  ```
  schedule_id|event_id|session_title|session_time|duration_minutes|speaker|venue_id
  ```
- Description: Stores schedule sessions with timing, speakers, linked to events and venues
- Example Rows:
  ```
  1|1|Opening Keynote|2025-03-15 09:00|60|John Smith|1
  2|1|Panel Discussion|2025-03-15 10:30|90|Expert Panel|1
  3|2|Headliner Performance|2025-06-20 20:00|120|International Artist|2
  ```

---

This design specification strictly adheres to the user requirements. It enables independent frontend and backend development without assumptions or undocumented features.

# Design Specification for RealEstate Web Application

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification (Backend)

| Route Path                 | HTTP Method | Function Name           | Template Rendered         | Route Parameters         | Context Variables Passed to Template (name : type) | POST Form Data Expected (name : type)                                  |
|----------------------------|-------------|------------------------|---------------------------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
| /                          | GET         | root_redirect           | Redirects to /dashboard   | None                     | None                                               | None                                                                 |
| /dashboard                 | GET         | dashboard              | dashboard.html            | None                     | featured_properties : list of dict                         | None                                                                 |
| /properties                | GET         | property_search        | property_search.html       | None                     | properties : list of dict                                 | None                                                                 |
| /property/<int:property_id>| GET         | property_details       | property_details.html      | property_id (int)        | property : dict                                         | None                                                                 |
| /inquiry                   | GET         | inquiry_form           | inquiry.html               | None                     | properties : list of dict                               | None                                                                 |
| /inquiry                   | POST        | submit_inquiry         | Redirect or inquiry.html   | None                     | errors : dict (optional), properties : list of dict     | property_id : int, customer_name : str, customer_email : str,
customer_phone : str, message : str                          |
| /inquiries                | GET         | inquiries_list         | inquiries.html             | None                     | inquiries : list of dict                               | None                                                                 |
| /inquiries/delete/<int:inquiry_id> | POST        | delete_inquiry          | Redirect or inquiries.html | inquiry_id (int)        | inquiries : list of dict (if re-rendering)              | None                                                                 |
| /favorites                | GET         | favorites_list         | favorites.html             | None                     | favorite_properties : list of dict                      | None                                                                 |
| /favorites/add/<int:property_id> | POST        | add_favorite           | Redirect to favorites      | property_id (int)        | None                                               | None                                                                 |
| /favorites/remove/<int:property_id> | POST        | remove_favorite        | Redirect to favorites      | property_id (int)        | None                                               | None                                                                 |
| /agents                   | GET         | agents_list            | agents.html                | None                     | agents : list of dict                                   | None                                                                 |
| /locations                | GET         | locations_list         | locations.html             | None                     | locations : list of dict                               | None                                                                 |


### Details

#### root_redirect
- Redirects from '/' to '/dashboard'

#### dashboard
- Renders dashboard.html
- Context Variable: featured_properties (list of dict), each dict representing featured property details

#### property_search
- Renders property_search.html
- Context Variable: properties (list of dict) all available properties

#### property_details
- Parameter: property_id (int)
- Renders property_details.html
- Context Variable: property (dict) with all property details

#### inquiry_form (GET)
- Renders inquiry.html
- Context Variable: properties (list of dict) to populate the select-property dropdown

#### submit_inquiry (POST)
- Accepts form data:
  - property_id (int)
  - customer_name (str)
  - customer_email (str)
  - customer_phone (str)
  - message (str)
- On success redirects to inquiries list or confirmation
- On failure re-renders inquiry.html with errors context

#### inquiries_list
- Renders inquiries.html
- Context Variable: inquiries (list of dict) all inquiries

#### delete_inquiry (POST)
- Parameter: inquiry_id (int) to delete
- Redirects to /inquiries

#### favorites_list
- Renders favorites.html
- Context Variable: favorite_properties (list of dict) all favorite properties data

#### add_favorite (POST)
- Parameter: property_id (int)
- Adds favorite and redirects to /favorites

#### remove_favorite (POST)
- Parameter: property_id (int)
- Removes favorite and redirects to /favorites

#### agents_list
- Renders agents.html
- Context Variable: agents (list of dict) all agents data

#### locations_list
- Renders locations.html
- Context Variable: locations (list of dict) all locations data

---

## Section 2: HTML Templates (Frontend)

### 1. dashboard.html
- Page Title: Real Estate Dashboard
- Element IDs:
  - dashboard-page (Div)
  - featured-properties (Div)
  - browse-properties-button (Button)
  - my-inquiries-button (Button)
  - my-favorites-button (Button)
- Context Variables:
  - featured_properties (list of dict) - each dict contains property info (id, address, price, etc.)
- Navigation:
  - browse-properties-button: url_for('property_search')
  - my-inquiries-button: url_for('inquiries_list')
  - my-favorites-button: url_for('favorites_list')
- Forms: None

### 2. property_search.html
- Page Title: Property Search
- Element IDs:
  - search-page (Div)
  - location-input (Input text)
  - price-range-min (Input number)
  - price-range-max (Input number)
  - property-type-filter (Dropdown)
  - properties-grid (Div)
  - view-property-button-{property_id} (Button per property)
- Context Variables:
  - properties (list of dict) - all properties with details
- Navigation:
  - view-property-button-{property_id}: url_for('property_details', property_id=property_id)
- Forms: None (search input filtering handled client side or via query params)

### 3. property_details.html
- Page Title: Property Details
- Element IDs:
  - property-details-page (Div)
  - property-address (H1)
  - property-price (Div)
  - property-description (Div)
  - property-features (Div)
  - add-to-favorites-button (Button)
  - submit-inquiry-button (Button)
- Context Variables:
  - property (dict) with keys: property_id, address, price, description, bedrooms, bathrooms, square_feet
- Navigation:
  - add-to-favorites-button: form POST to url_for('add_favorite', property_id=property['property_id'])
  - submit-inquiry-button: url_for('inquiry_form') (likely with property selected pre-filled if client-side handled)
- Forms:
  - Add to favorites form with submit button add-to-favorites-button

### 4. inquiry.html
- Page Title: Submit Property Inquiry
- Element IDs:
  - inquiry-page (Div)
  - select-property (Dropdown)
  - inquiry-name (Input)
  - inquiry-email (Input email)
  - inquiry-phone (Input tel)
  - inquiry-message (Textarea)
  - submit-inquiry-button (Button)
- Context Variables:
  - properties (list of dict) for select-property dropdown
  - errors (dict) optional for validation errors
- Navigation:
  - None
- Forms:
  - Inquiry form POST to url_for('submit_inquiry') with fields:
    - select-property (property_id)
    - inquiry-name
    - inquiry-email
    - inquiry-phone
    - inquiry-message
    - Submit button id: submit-inquiry-button

### 5. inquiries.html
- Page Title: My Inquiries
- Element IDs:
  - inquiries-page (Div)
  - inquiries-table (Table)
  - inquiry-status-filter (Dropdown)
  - delete-inquiry-button-{inquiry_id} (Button per inquiry)
  - back-to-dashboard (Button)
- Context Variables:
  - inquiries (list of dict) each with inquiry details
- Navigation:
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')
- Forms:
  - Delete inquiry forms POST to url_for('delete_inquiry', inquiry_id=inquiry_id)

### 6. favorites.html
- Page Title: My Favorite Properties
- Element IDs:
  - favorites-page (Div)
  - favorites-list (Div)
  - remove-from-favorites-button-{property_id} (Button per property)
  - view-property-button-{property_id} (Button per property)
  - back-to-dashboard (Button)
- Context Variables:
  - favorite_properties (list of dict) all favorite properties details
- Navigation:
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')
  - view-property-button-{property_id}: url_for('property_details', property_id=property_id)
- Forms:
  - Remove from favorites forms POST to url_for('remove_favorite', property_id=property_id)

### 7. agents.html
- Page Title: Real Estate Agents
- Element IDs:
  - agents-page (Div)
  - agents-list (Div)
  - agent-search (Input)
  - contact-agent-button-{agent_id} (Button per agent)
  - back-to-dashboard (Button)
- Context Variables:
  - agents (list of dict) each containing agent details
- Navigation:
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')
- Forms:
  - contact-agent-button-{agent_id} (Button) action undefined (informational, no POST specified)

### 8. locations.html
- Page Title: Featured Locations
- Element IDs:
  - locations-page (Div)
  - locations-list (Div)
  - view-location-button-{location_id} (Button per location)
  - location-sort (Dropdown)
  - back-to-dashboard (Button)
- Context Variables:
  - locations (list of dict) all location details
- Navigation:
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard')
- Forms:
  - None (sorting/filtering client side or via query)

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas (Backend)

### 1. Properties Data
- File Path: data/properties.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - property_id
  - address
  - location
  - price
  - property_type
  - bedrooms
  - bathrooms
  - square_feet
  - description
  - agent_id
  - status
- Description: Stores detailed information about all properties in the system.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|123 Oak Street|Downtown|450000|House|3|2|2500|Beautiful family home with large yard|101|Available
  - 2|456 Park Avenue|Midtown|350000|Apartment|2|1.5|1200|Modern apartment with city view|102|Available
  - 3|789 Elm Road|Suburb|280000|Condo|2|2|1500|Cozy condo in quiet neighborhood|101|Sold

### 2. Locations Data
- File Path: data/locations.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - location_id
  - location_name
  - region
  - average_price
  - property_count
  - description
- Description: Stores information about locations including average price and property count.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|Downtown|Central|425000|45|Urban area with business district
  - 2|Midtown|Central|380000|38|Mixed residential and commercial zone
  - 3|Suburb|Outskirts|295000|52|Family-friendly residential area

### 3. Property Inquiries Data
- File Path: data/inquiries.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - inquiry_id
  - property_id
  - customer_name
  - customer_email
  - customer_phone
  - message
  - inquiry_date
  - status
- Description: Stores user inquiries about properties with contact details and inquiry status.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|John Smith|john@email.com|555-1234|Interested in viewing this weekend|2025-01-15|Pending
  - 2|2|Sarah Johnson|sarah@email.com|555-5678|Can we schedule a showing?|2025-01-16|Contacted
  - 3|1|Mike Davis|mike@email.com|555-9012|What is the lowest offer?|2025-01-17|Resolved

### 4. Favorite Properties Data
- File Path: data/favorites.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - favorite_id
  - property_id
  - added_date
- Description: Stores user favorite property references with dates added.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|2025-01-10
  - 2|2|2025-01-12
  - 3|3|2025-01-14

### 5. Real Estate Agents Data
- File Path: data/agents.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - agent_id
  - agent_name
  - specialization
  - email
  - phone
  - properties_sold
- Description: Stores real estate agent details including specialization and contact.
- Example Rows:
  - 101|Robert Wilson|Residential Properties|robert@email.com|555-0001|125
  - 102|Emily Chen|Commercial Real Estate|emily@email.com|555-0002|89
  - 103|James Martinez|Luxury Homes|james@email.com|555-0003|67

---

# End of design_spec.md

  - 2|Midtown|Central|380000|38|Mixed residential and commercial zone
  - 3|Suburb|Outskirts|295000|52|Family-friendly residential area

### 3. Property Inquiries Data
- File Path: data/inquiries.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - inquiry_id
  - property_id
  - customer_name
  - customer_email
  - customer_phone
  - message
  - inquiry_date
  - status
- Description: Stores user inquiries about properties with contact details and inquiry status.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|John Smith|john@email.com|555-1234|Interested in viewing this weekend|2025-01-15|Pending
  - 2|2|Sarah Johnson|sarah@email.com|555-5678|Can we schedule a showing?|2025-01-16|Contacted
  - 3|1|Mike Davis|mike@email.com|555-9012|What is the lowest offer?|2025-01-17|Resolved

### 4. Favorite Properties Data
- File Path: data/favorites.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - favorite_id
  - property_id
  - added_date
- Description: Stores user favorite property references with dates added.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|2025-01-10
  - 2|2|2025-01-12
  - 3|3|2025-01-14

### 5. Real Estate Agents Data
- File Path: data/agents.txt
- Pipe-delimited Fields (in order):
  - agent_id
  - agent_name
  - specialization
  - email
  - phone
  - properties_sold
- Description: Stores real estate agent details including specialization and contact.
- Example Rows:
  - 101|Robert Wilson|Residential Properties|robert@email.com|555-0001|125
  - 102|Emily Chen|Commercial Real Estate|emily@email.com|555-0002|89
  - 103|James Martinez|Luxury Homes|james@email.com|555-0003|67

---

# End of design_spec.md
